title: Setup and Installation
description: Test your knowledge of Playwright installation and project setup

questions.question: Complete the npm command to install Playwright:
questions.explanation: Use 'npm install -D' or 'npm i -D' to install Playwright as a development dependency.

questions.question: What command initializes a new Playwright project?
questions.explanation: The command 'npm init playwright@latest' initializes a new Playwright project with recommended setup.

questions.question: Playwright automatically downloads browser binaries during installation.
questions.explanation: Playwright downloads the necessary browser binaries (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit) automatically during installation.

questions.question: Which file contains Playwright configuration settings?
questions.explanation: Playwright uses 'playwright.config.ts' (or .js) as the main configuration file for test settings.

questions.question: Complete the command to install only Chromium browser:
questions.explanation: Use 'npx playwright install chromium' to install only the Chromium browser binary.

questions.question: Node.js is required to run Playwright tests.
questions.explanation: Playwright requires Node.js version 16 or higher to run JavaScript/TypeScript tests.

questions.question: What is the minimum Node.js version required for Playwright?
questions.explanation: Playwright requires Node.js version 16 or higher.

questions.question: What's the issue with this installation command?
questions.explanation: The correct package name is '@playwright/test' and it should be installed as a dev dependency with -D flag.

questions.question: Which command shows the installed Playwright version?
questions.explanation: You can check the Playwright version using 'npx playwright --version' or 'npm list @playwright/test'.

questions.question: You need to install each browser separately when using Playwright.
questions.explanation: Playwright installs all supported browsers (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit) by default unless specified otherwise.
